segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a dynamic montage of a triathlete in action, accompanied by intense, driving music and crowd cheers. A male voice (the Triathlete) declares, "Today is very important day," followed by motivational shouts like "Finally eight months!" and "Let's go, man, come on!" as he swims in a crowded open-water race, transitions to a focused bike ride, and finally runs at night with a medal. A sharp sound effect marks a transition to a calm, sunlit morning at a yellow house. The Triathlete, now casually dressed in a white t-shirt, stands on a patio, speaking directly to the camera, "Alright guys, welcome to a new video. In 24 hours, it will be the start of our very first full Ironman." On-screen text reads 'BREAKFAST 06:45 | 24 HOURS UNTIL START.' He continues, "We're here in Klagenfurt. Looks very beautiful. Today is a very important day. We need to eat well, carb up as they say. We have a big planning today." He walks to a table, changes into a light blue hoodie, and sits down to eat a bowl of cereal with another man, also in a matching hoodie. The Second Man greets him, "Good morning. Something different about you." The Triathlete replies, "Yeah, I don't know what it is," before revealing, "I cut my hair." The Second Man exclaims, "Oh, that's it! I know you look sharp," to which the Triathlete responds, "I hope so. It's tomorrow." The Second Man adds, "You can be proud." The Triathlete then outlines the day's schedule, gesturing animatedly while eating, mentioning "Race briefing at 9, open water swim training at 10, transition bag preparations with Freya at 12."

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with the Triathlete, a man with dark curly hair and a mustache, and the Second Man, in matching light blue hoodies, seated at a wooden table outdoors. They share breakfast, animatedly discussing the race, with one of them noting they'll realize "we're fucked" at the transition zone. Both men then enthusiastically exclaim, "carb the fuck up!" as they prepare. The scene transitions to the Triathlete, now in a white hoodie with a red and black backpack, carrying a water bottle, walking through a bustling outdoor event area. One of the men remarks, "On our way to the race briefing. We're semi-late." On-screen text confirms 'RACE BRIEFING 09:07 | 21 HOURS UNTIL START'. He navigates through a crowd of athletes and event staff, approaching a large stage where a race briefing is underway. The crowd cheers and applauds energetically. The stage features a large screen displaying 'IRONMAN KLAGENFURT AUSTRIA' and a countdown timer. A Race Briefing Speaker, a man in a dark shirt, addresses the attentive crowd with a microphone, humorously advising them to "sit down, you need to save your legs for tomorrow." He explains that "3,000 people doing this race" will experience "race brain," defining it as "the most intelligent people on the planet become stupid when they put on lycra," adding "It's normal." The Triathlete and the Second Man are seen standing together, listening intently and smiling. The speaker continues, emphasizing that the briefing's purpose is for everyone to "hear the words 'you are an Ironman'," which elicits a collective "yes" from the crowd. He then points to a map, highlighting the Wörthersee as the starting point, noting its "so clean" water, as the energetic and anticipatory atmosphere continues under bright daylight.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens on a covered outdoor patio where the Shirtless Man at Table, already wearing a white and black cycling helmet marked '1322' and 'IRONMAN', unpacks a red 'MAM' bag. A male voice, identified as his, describes the contents, listing "helmets," "run bag," "shoes," "extra socks," "caps I can choose from," and "extra power," adding that "Tomorrow, extra water, extra gels in it. He then puts on a white cycling jersey and sunglasses, interacting with the Second Man, who wears a black t-shirt with a red stripe and a blue cap, as they prepare gear at a table with bicycles and bags. Upbeat, motivational electronic music begins to play as he walks to a rack of numbered 'Bike Gear Bags' and retrieves his bag labeled '1322'. The male voice (Shirtless Man) reflects on the impending race, stating, "Now it's all out of our hands. It's actually not out of our hands. We still have to do it. Tomorrow, it is time. We will become Iron Man or Wooden Boy," adding with a touch of humor, "If I fail, I tattoo Wooden Boy. Tomorrow in the morning, I suggest you..." as the minute concludes with the palpable pre-race energy.

The Shirtless Man, now wearing a white t-shirt with 'Stop Working Start Drinking' printed on the back, is seen on a balcony, smiling and reacting positively, playfully responding, "Stop, stop. This is not true. You're teasing, you're teasing." The scene alternates between the smartphone screen and the Shirtless Man, who is standing on a balcony with a yellow wall and metal railing, overlooking a green lawn and residential houses under a dusky sky. He is joined by another man, also in a white t-shirt, who listens intently. The Shirtless Man expresses gratitude to Cody, saying, "Cody, thanks for calling, thanks for checking in. We will think of you when we run." Cody responds, "Yeah, please do. Think of me, think of me," and then emphatically adds, "Keep going! Energy is everything!" The Shirtless Man concludes the call with, "All right, Cody thanks. Big love." After the call ends, the Shirtless Man turns to his companion, gesturing and speaking animatedly, summarizing Cody's advice: "You heard the final advice. I think now it's time to go to bed, get some good rest and tomorrow 4 AM wake up." He then humorously adds, "I'm ready to get it over and drink tequila and get wasted and puke on everything that's moving." His companion responds, "That's motivation. See you tomorrow. Good night," to which the Shirtless Man replies, "Good night." A friendly kiss sound is heard as they share a moment of camaraderie, before the scene transitions to darkness, and ambient, slightly melancholic music begins to play. The scene then cuts to the Shirtless Man entering a warmly lit kitchen, holding a white bag and a phone, appearing focused. The kitchen features wooden cabinets, a clock on the wall, and a stove. His voice is heard in a reflective, slightly muffled tone, stating, "Iron Man day. Didn't sleep well. But that's expected. But I'm feeling good. Feeling energized," as the ambient music continues softly in the background.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a man in a white hoodie and black pants unpacking a variety of snacks and drinks from a plastic bag onto a wooden table on a balcony, with a scenic view of green hills and houses in the background, as gentle ambient music begins and a voice declares, "Today's the day." He then transitions to a bustling outdoor event area, where he walks alongside a bald man in a black jacket and beige shorts, carrying a white bag with orange branding, while indistinct chatter and the ambient music continue, and a voice notes, "We're leaving soon." They navigate through a crowded bike parking area filled with numerous bicycles, including a prominent blue Canyon bike with race number 1322. The man in the white hoodie, now wearing a red and black backpack, is seen attaching a water bottle to his bike and later walking along a red carpeted path with the bald man, exchanging French greetings like "Ah, Fredric!" and "Ça va?" with others. As they move through a tree-lined path, they join a larger group of athletes preparing for an event, many wearing wetsuits and swim caps, while the bald man is heard requesting "twee eh, iso en ene water," discussing hydration. The man in the white hoodie is then shown putting on a black wetsuit with 'Z3R0D' branding, adjusting a bright yellow swim cap, and wearing colorful goggles, smiling confidently. During this, the bald man announces, "22 degrees the water temp," to which the Triathlete responds, "Oh, really? Okay," before admitting, "I'm a little bit of stress." The bald man reassures him, "That's normal," and the Triathlete affirms, "You have to, you have to finish, for sure." Laughter is heard amidst the preparations, followed by the bald man announcing, "30 minutes before the race starts." He then asks the Triathlete, "How are you feeling?" to which he replies, "Stressed but composed," prompting the bald man to remark, "Oh, that's a combination that is very rare." The atmosphere is lively and energetic, with participants milling about, some stretching, others chatting, under the soft golden light of early morning, accompanied by the general hum of the event. The Triathlete mentions putting on his wetsuit, to which the bald man urges, "So snel mogelijk" (as fast as possible), and the Triathlete confirms, "It's a half." The swim start is announced to be in 15 minutes, as the crowd erupts in energetic cheering and shouts of "Come on!"

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with an announcer's voice declaring that triathletes from several countries are taking to the water, accompanied by a background crowd murmur. The Triathlete, now identified as Rob, fully geared in his black 'Z3R0D' and 'MMAX' wetsuit, bright yellow swim cap, and colorful goggles, receives last-minute encouragement from the Second Man. The Second Man, a bald man in a black jacket, adjusts Rob's wetsuit, pats his shoulder, and offers motivational words in a supportive, slightly accented voice, saying, 'Just breathe,' 'Just enjoy today, you'll do great,' and 'You worked for this, you got it, just pick up the medal at the finish line.' Rob, visibly emotional, responds with a slightly nervous but determined voice, saying, 'Let's go, yes,' and 'Proud of you too,' before they share a heartfelt embrace. The Second Man continues to advise, 'Don't start too fast, eh?' and 'Focus on eating and drinking. Hydration. This is the last time we see each other before the end, okay? I want to see you at the finish, okay?' Rob replies, 'Come on, bro,' as the Second Man adds, 'Let's go. You can do this, eh?' Rob then walks away, joining a group of other athletes in wetsuits and green swim caps, all heading toward the swim start. As he moves through the crowd, passing event staff and spectators, dramatic, uplifting instrumental music begins to build anticipation. He approaches the water’s edge under a large 'RŌKA' arch. The scene shifts to a wide aerial view of the swim start, where hundreds of athletes in green swim caps are entering the water, creating a dynamic splash. The announcer's voice returns, stating, 'And here they are. Rob is on his start line.' The Second Man is later seen clapping and smiling from the shore, clearly proud. Rob, now at the start line, looks focused and determined, with the on-screen text 'Eight months of training, for this,' which is also spoken by the announcer, emphasizing the culmination of his preparation. The atmosphere is charged with anticipation, set against a backdrop of green trees, clear skies, and the calm lake, with event branding visible throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a vibrant and energetic scene at the Ironman swim start, likely at the Wörthersee, immediately filled with the sounds of an excited crowd and upbeat background music. A man in a blue polo shirt and cap gestures enthusiastically as a swimmer in a black wetsuit with 'Z3R0D' branding and a bright green swim cap runs into the water, met with loud cheers and shouts of "Let's go Rob! Let's go man, come on! Let's go!" A commentator's voice confirms, "And there he goes, Rob is on his way..." The camera follows the swimmer, then transitions to a wider shot showing multiple athletes in colorful green, blue, and yellow caps entering the lake under a 'RŌKA' branded arch. The focus then shifts to 'Arno the Kid,' identified by on-screen text and the commentator asking, "And where is Arno the Kid?" Arno, in a blue swim cap and a black wetsuit with red accents, smiles, makes a peace sign, and exclaims in his distinct voice, "Finally, eight months. Eight months!" before running into the water amidst more cheers and shouts of "Let's go baby!" and an enthusiastic "Let's fucking go, man! Let's go!" The commentator further identifies him as "the younger brother of Rob, Arno the Kid." Arno is heard saying "Relax. Thank you." as he enters the water, cheered on by the crowd, including a woman in a light blue sweater waving a red-and-white flag. As the swimmers begin their race, the sounds of splashing water mix with the continuing music and crowd noise. Dynamic shots capture them swimming freestyle in the turquoise water, creating splashes and moving in a pack, while spectators line the banks and a wooden bridge overhead, some waving flags and others capturing the moment on their phones. The bright, sunny setting with lush green trees and visible event branding like 'RŌKA,' 'Gatorade,' and 'HOKA' reinforces the atmosphere of excitement and community, culminating in loud cheers of "Go Arno!" as the commentator notes, "Go Arno, yeah, he wants to win here."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a dynamic shot of swimmers entering a turquoise lake, creating splashes amidst enthusiastic cheers and shouts from spectators. A woman in a light blue sweater waves a red-and-white flag, adding to the energetic atmosphere. The camera then shifts to the shore, where a bald man in a black jacket, identified as the Second Man, is seen smiling and observing the swimmers, with on-screen text noting, 'He's still smiling. He's fully at ease.' A voice in Dutch/Flemish is heard remarking, "He can lachen" (He can laugh), possibly referring to the Second Man. A male commentator then provides an update on the Triathlete's performance, stating, "He's doing good, he's swimming really comfortable, 48 minutes now, so I think his finishing time in swim will be around one hour," adding that "he's swimming from group to group, so he has a nice pace, everything under control." Splashing sounds intensify as the Triathlete, in a black wetsuit with 'Z3R0D' and 'MMAX' branding, a bright yellow swim cap, and colorful goggles, emerges from the water. He is greeted by volunteers in orange shirts labeled 'Volunteer Team' who assist him onto a red carpet path, while spectators continue to cheer loudly, with one voice shouting, "Ah, come on up!" As he walks through the transition area, passing under a 'RŌKA' arch and past banners for 'KÄRNTEN' and 'FULZ', German voices from the crowd are heard discussing a cramp, "Jawohl, jawohl, da merkt man schon. Krampf aber das geht dann schon." (Yes, yes, you can tell. Cramp, but it will pass.) A volunteer warns the Triathlete in Dutch/Flemish, "Pas op, niet uitglijden, niet uitglijden, het is super glad" (Watch out, don't slip, don't slip, it's super slippery), while another voice encourages, "Let's go, let's go, let's go. Ja, voilà, nu zijt ge safe." (Yes? Let's go... Yes, there, now you're safe.) The Triathlete, visibly wet and focused, continues walking with determination, passing by a blue banner with 'Santini' and 'Hyperbar' logos, and eventually enters a covered area where the lighting dims slightly. More cheers erupt, with a spectator shouting, "Hey hey, guys, good luck guys!" The segment concludes with a male commentator reflecting, "It was really nice actually. Everything was perfect. Okay, good. Really. It was beautiful. The morale is good." The atmosphere remains energetic and supportive throughout.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up, first-person view of Arno the Kid during the Ironman cycling leg, wearing a white 'TOUT BIEN' t-shirt, black helmet with race number '1322', and orange goggles. His face shows visible exertion as he speaks, his voice slightly strained and accompanied by heavy breathing, reflecting on the race's progress: "Man, the best eight months have gone so fast and now I'm doing it and I'm really enjoying it." He notes being "35 kilometers" in, then humorously recounts an incident where "somebody passed me, puts his ass in the air and just farts like crazy," which "lightened up the mood." He continues, stating "80 kilometers in," still "feeling good," though worried about not applying sunscreen and having lost a water bottle. Arno describes a "crazy downhill" where he "almost crashed" due to another participant's bidon rolling on the road, requiring "ninja moves" to avoid it. Despite these challenges, he feels "fresh, feeling good," and notes the "super nice" people and "a lot of ambiance" along the course, concluding with a determined "Let's go. Let's finish this Ironman." As he speaks, the camera shifts, revealing glimpses of the sky and roadside environment, including a streetlight and a no-turn sign. The scene then transitions to an energetic spectator area where a woman in a light blue sweater waves a red-and-white flag, among other supporters, their loud, enthusiastic cheers filling the audio. Finally, Arno arrives at a Gatorade station, now in a white and black tri-suit with 'average rob' and a red heart, where a volunteer in an orange 'Volunteer Team' shirt hands him a blue water bottle and a white towel amidst event banners like 'Santini' and 'Hyperbar', under bright daylight.

segments.integrated_caption: The Ironman Klagenfurt Austria cycling leg bursts with energy as spectators loudly cheer "Go, go, go!" and call out race numbers like "27, 27!" and "32, 32!". Arno the Kid, in his white and black 'TOUT BIEN' tri-suit, helmet '1322', and orange goggles, pulls into a Gatorade station. He thanks the 'Volunteer Team' in bright orange shirts as they hand him a blue water bottle and a white towel, with one volunteer possibly remarking, "It's a lot, eh?". Despite visible exertion, Arno maintains focus, confirming he's "feeling good, feeling energized" and that his race is "Ça va, ça va? Pour ma course?" (going well). The lively crowd continues to cheer, shouting "Woo!" and "Yeah!". A man in a navy blue 'CERATIZIT' shirt, acting as a commentator, informs the camera that "Arno is now 2 kilometers away from the personal needs station," expecting him in "two, three minutes." As Arno continues, other cyclists, including one in a black 'BIEN' tri-suit, also stop for hydration and encouragement. Spectators call out "Arno, Arno, Arno!" and offer advice like "Yo, chill. We gotta take it chill. There's a marathon after," emphasizing the strategic nature of the race. The sunny weather and lush green scenery underscore the competitive outdoor environment, with the distant yellow 'BILLA' building serving as a landmark amidst the ongoing cheers and race commentary.

segments.integrated_caption: Arno the Kid, clad in a white and black tri-suit with 'TOUT BIEN' and a red heart, a white cycling helmet marked '1322', and orange-tinted goggles, is visibly fatigued during the latter stages of the Ironman cycling leg. He stops at a Gatorade station, where volunteers in bright orange shirts assist him with hydration and a banana. A volunteer asks about someone behind, and Arno, despite his exhaustion, responds, "I am a little bit getting tired, I feel it in my back, but uh, I'm I'm doing good. I'm very happy." The volunteer encourages him, saying he looks "fresh" and asking if he's "enjoying it," to which Arno replies, "Mmm, it's really nice." After being wished "Good luck" by a volunteer and hearing a shout of "Let's go!", Arno thanks them and resumes riding. As he pedals, passing other cyclists including a second one in a black tri-suit with 'BIEN' and a red heart, he speaks directly to the camera, stating he's "130k in" and his "legs are starting to feel weak," feeling like he's "losing power." He hopes it "doesn't go bad" and pushes on, mentioning "140 km, 150..." with strained breathing. He declares, "The marathon is gonna be, it's gonna be a war," with "30 more kilometers to go." The setting shifts from a tree-lined road with spectators and event staff to open countryside under a bright blue sky, with distant hills and a yellow building with a 'BILLA' sign. Arno anticipates the "last climb" before a "15 kilometers down to the finish" and then the marathon, asking for luck amidst crowd cheering. Two other male voices are heard discussing another cyclist, 'Rob', noting he's "doing pretty fast" but will likely "start slowing down."

segments.integrated_caption: The video segment opens with a close-up of Arno the Kid’s legs as he pushes his blue bicycle, race number 1322, along a paved road, transitioning from the swim to the bike leg of the Ironman. He is now wearing a white t-shirt with 'TOUT BIEN' printed in red letters, a white cycling helmet with race number '1322', and orange-tinted goggles. His voice, slightly strained and accompanied by heavy breathing, is heard saying, "I'm taking a chill" and "Cheers, I'll see you." He then speaks directly to the camera, expressing exhaustion at kilometer 170, admitting, "I'm so tired actually... my legs now are quite tired. I might have underestimated this." Despite this, he remains determined, declaring, "we keep going, we keep pushing, and 'on met la patate' that's the most important. Come on, let's go." The camera captures his strained yet resolute expression under the bright sun, with trees and a distant lake visible in the background. He then stops at a Gatorade station, where volunteers in orange shirts hand him a blue water bottle and a white towel. After hydrating, he continues riding, passing under a black inflatable arch labeled 'BIKE OUT', marking the official start of the cycling leg. The scene shifts to a red carpet path lined with an enthusiastic Event Crowd whose loud cheers, clapping, and shouts of "Let's go, Arno!", "Come on, man!", and "Come on, boy!" fill the air, creating an energetic and supportive atmosphere. Banners from sponsors like 'FULGAZ' and 'QATAR AIRWAYS' are visible. Arno the Kid pushes his bike along the red carpet, passing a 'BIKE OUT' sign, and eventually stops to check his watch, his voice again reflecting fatigue, "My batteries are empty. I can't imagine doing a marathon now," before reaffirming his resolve, "But I'm good, I'm good. I'm doing good." The environment is vibrant and sunny, with lush green trees, clear skies, and event branding visible throughout, emphasizing the high-energy atmosphere of the Ironman event.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens in a clinical office where a Doctor, in a white short-sleeved shirt, holds a foot model and delivers a diagnosis to the Triathlete, a man with dark curly hair and a mustache. The Triathlete reacts with a pained "Oef. Ooh." as the Doctor touches his foot, and a supportive male voice says "Saffi man." The Doctor acknowledges the Triathlete's determination to compete, warning him he'll "pay a price afterwards," to which the Triathlete responds "Okay." segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with Arno the Kid, now in a black tri-suit with a red heart and 'TOUT BIEN' on the back, bending over to adjust his gear at a transition area marked by orange benches and branded banners like 'wahoo' and 'ACTIVE'. A man in a gray shirt with sunglasses offers strategic advice, saying, "call me for the aid station, so we split up. One, two and a half, walk through the aid station, drink, and then you go off and you run again. Okay. Rob is doing exactly the same thing and he's doing perfect, yeah." Arno then stands up, takes a drink from a red and white Gatorade bottle, and begins running along a paved path lined with metal barriers and cheering spectators. As he runs, he passes a Gatorade station where volunteers in orange shirts hand him a blue water bottle and a white towel, amidst loud crowd cheering and shouts of "Come on!" A voice encourages him, "Easy, easy, easy, easy, easy, easy, easy... Very easy. Yes, yes, that's better. Come on, man. Up!" Another supporter wishes him, "Good luck, boy," to which Arno replies, "Thank you." As he continues, a voice exclaims, "Let's fucking go, my guy," followed by another saying, "He's a so good bezig. Halfway. Halfway there, let's go," amidst renewed intense cheering. Arno transitions into a tree-lined path, high-fiving a volunteer and passing under a bridge with a 'VANOS OPA' banner. The camera then shifts to a close-up of his determined face, under a white visor and orange-tinted sunglasses, as he speaks to the camera, revealing his mental strategy: "Every time I feel pain, I tell myself it's not pain. And then it goes away." He adds, "You're gonna take one more painkiller after? Let's go run! Let's go! And then I'm gonna finish it. Still going. Still running. I will not stop running. Come on. Only 14 kilometers left. And then we're Ironman. Come on. So Ironman." The background reveals a suburban setting with green trees, houses, and other runners in the distance, emphasizing the community and energy of the race.

segments.integrated_caption: The video captures a dynamic segment of Arno the Kid’s Ironman race, transitioning from the cycling leg into the run. He is seen running along a paved path bordered by greenery and event barriers, wearing a white and black tri-suit with 'TOUT BIEN' and a red heart on the back, a white visor, and orange-tinted sunglasses. His race number, 1322, is clearly visible on his waist. As he runs, his internal monologue reveals his struggle, admitting it's "beginning to getting heavy," but his determination shines through as he states, "I have to finish." He passes spectators and volunteers, including a group in orange shirts labeled 'Volunteer Team' at a Gatorade station, where he receives a blue water bottle and a white towel. He interacts warmly with the volunteers, giving high-fives and smiling, showing his energy and engagement, while the crowd's enthusiastic shouts of "Come on Arno!", "Allez!", and "Let's go!" fill the air. One volunteer encourages him with "You're doing a great job!", to which Arno responds with "Thank you, thank you." A man in a gray shirt and sunglasses by the roadside also offers strong encouragement, telling Arno, "You're fucking great." Arno laughs, and his internal thoughts continue, noting "the painkillers are doing excellent job" and expressing his emotional connection to the race, stating, "Every time I see them, I get emotional," and even "I cry a little" when thinking about the finish. The environment is bright and sunny, with lush trees, grassy fields, and event signage like 'KÄRNTEN' and 'FULZ' banners lining the route. He continues running past cheering spectators, some waving flags and others filming, maintaining a steady pace while occasionally glancing at his watch, emphasizing the endurance and community spirit of the race.

segments.integrated_caption: The video captures the final, emotional stretch of Rob Van Impe's Ironman run, transitioning from a tree-lined park path to a residential street and finally to the finish line, all under bright, warm late afternoon sunlight casting long shadows. Rob, explicitly identified by on-screen text and a race announcer, is seen wearing a white tri-suit with 'TOUT BIEN' and a red heart, a white visor with 'COMPRESS' branding, and orange-tinted sunglasses. He is visibly sweaty, displaying a determined yet joyful expression, occasionally smiling at the camera. Throughout his run, the enthusiastic Event Crowd lines the route behind barriers, cheering loudly with shouts of "So close!", "Let's go, boys!", and "Come on!", waving flags and holding signs, their applause intensifying as he progresses, punctuated by a brief siren-like sound. Rob himself is heard expressing deep emotion, stating he's "cried like four times already" and finding the experience "so emotional," noting "only two more kilometers" with disbelief and an emotional exhale. As he enters the final 100m, on-screen text confirms his identity as Rob Van Impe, trained by World Champions Luc and Frederik Van Lierde, a detail echoed by a professional Race Announcer who builds excitement, declaring, "He's into the final 100 meters, Rob Van Impe... And here he comes. Not so average anymore." The environment shifts from lush parkland to a suburban street with houses and trees, then to a more crowded finish area adorned with branded banners like 'VINFAST' and 'KÄRNTEN'. Rob crosses the large black inflatable 'VINFAST' Finish Line Arch, raising his arms in a triumphant expression as the crowd erupts in applause, marking the culmination of his challenging race.

He is then presented with a blue finisher’s medal by a man in a white shirt and celebrates with a fellow runner, both wearing medals and smiling broadly. The scene is filled with joy, exhaustion, and triumph, underscored by playful banter in a foreign language from close supporters, who tease him with "Wat doet 'em, dikke?" segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with the emotional climax of Arno the Kid’s Ironman journey as he crosses the finish line at night, visibly exhausted yet triumphant, amidst a swell of triumphant music, cheering, and applause from the event crowd. He is immediately embraced by a man in a gray shirt with a white visor and a '2023 IRONMAN FINISHER' medal, who exclaims, "Oh my god, I'm so proud of you, dude. I love you!" Arno, wearing a black tri-suit with 'arno the kid' and a red heart, responds with tears and a smile, "I love you, dude. We fucking did it, dude. Eight months," a sentiment echoed by the man in gray. The man humorously adds, "We're gonna get fat again. I can't wait to get fat." Arno then turns to kiss a blonde woman in a gray top, who hugs him tightly, excitedly shouting, "Fucking Arno, the fucking Ironman!" He then embraces an older man with gray hair, who, overwhelmed, says, "Oh my god, I love you, dude. Thank you for believing in me," and later, with a sigh, "Jezus, I love you." Arno replies in Dutch, "Dank je om in te geloven" (Thank you for believing in me), to which both the man in gray and the older man respond, "Always." The man in gray shirt lightens the mood, joking, "I'm stinky, yeah." The scene shifts to a celebratory moment as Arno and the man in the gray shirt face the camera, both wearing their finisher medals, with the man asking, "Should we do an outro?" and Arno agreeing. The man then addresses the camera, "Alright, guys, we did it. We finished it. People didn't believe in Arno in the beginning. And look at him now. What the fuck!" They smile, give a thumbs-up, and the on-screen text 'THUMBS UP SUBSCRIBE' appears, as the man begins a call to action. The background shows a dimly lit event area with banners reading 'THANK YOU FOR VISITING IRONMAN VILLAGE,' and spectators continue to cheer, clap, and film, creating an atmosphere of overwhelming joy, relief, and camaraderie, illuminated by artificial lights against the dark night sky.
